summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of Multi-state Compliance Phase 2 (TX/NY/CA/VA pre-seal evaluator) and Admin Ceremony Analytics dashboard. Backend pytest: 24/24 passed. All 5 evaluator-supported states wired (FL,TX,NY,CA,VA). TX/NY/CA/VA each return their distinct gate sets; NY correctly blocks will/codicil with 'NY prohibits RON for {type}'; CA includes thumbprint gate only for deed/POA/mortgage/real-property; FL returns redirect hint to /api/fl/ron/readiness. Unknown state (ZZ) returns 'No compliance abstract published'. Unauthenticated requests blocked (401/403). All 6 admin analytics endpoints (overview, funnel, timeseries, state-breakdown, top-notaries, gate-failures) return well-formed payloads for admin@notarychain.com (overview: 42 total, 9.5% completion, 30s avg TTS, $0 revenue; funnel pending=16, assigned=8, in_session=4, completed=4). All 6 endpoints correctly return 403 for demo@test.com (non-admin). timeseries?days=999 correctly 422-rejected. Frontend /admin/analytics under admin: KPI strip, daily area chart, funnel bar chart, gate-failures heatmap (showing 'Zero blocks · pipeline is healthy'), state breakdown table (UNSPECIFIED 42 / 4 sealed / 9.5%), top notaries (Platform Admin·3, John Notary·1) all rendered. 7d/30d/90d/180d window buttons reactive — clicking 90d refreshed the chart subtitle to 'last 90 days'. Non-admin (demo@test.com) navigation to /admin/analytics correctly shows 'Unable to load analytics — Admin access required' error card with 'Back to dashboard' button.

issues: React hydration warning in console: '<span> cannot be a child of <tbody>' — caused by the Emergent dev-tooling x-source wrapper (data-ve-dynamic span) being injected around <tr> elements inside <tbody>. This is dev-mode-only noise from the editor instrumentation, NOT a real code defect (the JSX in AdminCeremonyAnalytics.jsx is well-formed: tbody → tr.map). Does not affect production. Listed for awareness.

action_items: (none required) — All Phase 2 evaluator + analytics endpoints + dashboard verified working end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: services/multistate_evaluator.py: evaluator architecture is clean — STATE_EVALUATORS map cleanly dispatches per state, shared gate primitives (_check_kba, _check_av, _check_id_verification, _check_retention_tag, _check_journal_entry, _check_thumbprint, _check_principal_location) are composable. NY's special-case _block_all sentinel via reserved keys (_block_all, _blocked_reason) is a slightly fragile pattern (a future gate accidentally named '_block_all' would clash) — consider returning a structured BlockResult dataclass instead.

routes/compliance_phase2_routes.py:109 — `db.list_collection_names()` is called inside an aggregate guard for revenue calc. This adds a round-trip on every /overview call. Consider caching the collection-presence check or just always running the aggregate and treating empty result as $0 (faster, simpler).

_require_admin queries db.users by email on every admin-analytics request — fine at current scale, but if you add many admin endpoints consider stashing role on the decoded JWT instead so this is one DB read per request avoided.

Pre-seal evaluator gate primitives gracefully degrade when collections (biometric_thumbprints, ceremony_locations, identity_verifications) don't yet exist — they return passed=false rather than crashing. Verified in TX/CA/NY/VA tests.

AdminCeremonyAnalytics.jsx uses Promise.all to fan-out 6 GETs concurrently — good. The headers object is closed over inside useEffect; if `token` changes mid-session the captured headers wouldn't refresh until the next effect run. Effect deps array is empty (only mounts once) so a token refresh in-place wouldn't re-trigger. Low-risk since admin sessions are typically short-lived but worth noting.
Frontend uses `${process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL}/api` correctly — no hardcoded URLs.

Funnel endpoint uses snapshot-of-current-status (`db.notarization_requests.count_documents({status: s})`) and not status history transitions — so a ceremony currently in 'sealed' state is NOT counted under 'pending/assigned/in_session/completed' it previously passed through. The endpoint comment even acknowledges 'fallback to current status only'. This produces a funnel that is point-in-time accurate but NOT a true conversion funnel. Consider either renaming to 'status-distribution' or implementing status_history aggregation. Currently the UI shows 16/8/4/4/0/0 which understates the true 'pending → sealed' conversion because the 4 sealed ceremonies are NOT also counted in upstream stages.

data-testid coverage on /admin/analytics is excellent — kpi-strip, window-selector, window-{7,30,90,180}d, timeseries-card, funnel-card, gate-failures-card, gate-fail-{name}, state-breakdown-card, top-notaries-card, top-notary-{i}, back-admin-btn all present.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (admin dashboard fully rendered, all 6 cards populated, window selector reactive; non-admin denial path verified)

test_credentials: admin@notarychain.com / Admin123! (admin role); demo@test.com / Demo123! (regular user — returns 403 on all 6 admin endpoints; frontend shows 'Admin access required' card)
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data created. Tests used a transient ceremony_id (TEST_DUMMY_<uuid>) for evaluator gate checks — backend gracefully returns passed=false for missing collections rather than crashing, so no DB cleanup was needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_compliance_phase2_analytics.py covers: (a) evaluator/supported endpoint, (b) per-state gate sets for TX/NY/CA/VA, (c) NY will/codicil block, (d) CA thumbprint conditional on document_type, (e) FL redirect hint, (f) unknown state, (g) all 6 admin analytics endpoints with admin role + 403 for non-admin role. Auth uses /api/auth/login returning access_token. NOTE: User model from login does NOT carry .role attribute directly — the backend _require_admin helper does a DB lookup on users.email to fetch role. Frontend AuthContext likely hydrates role via /api/auth/me. If you need to test more evaluator paths, you'll need to seed records into kba_attempts/fl_av_quality_reports/ai_analyses/fl_retention_tags/fl_journal_entries/biometric_thumbprints/ceremony_locations to flip individual gates to passed=true. The funnel endpoint reports point-in-time status distribution (NOT a true conversion funnel) — see code-review comments.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional defects found. The console hydration warning on the state-breakdown table is a development-mode artifact from the Emergent editor instrumentation (data-ve-dynamic span wrappers around <tr> children), not a JSX defect in AdminCeremonyAnalytics.jsx.
